sections.label: Section 500
sections.original:
οὐδʼ ἥρπασεν χάρυβδις οἰωνοσκόπον ,
τέθριππον ἅρμα περιβαλοῦσα χάσματι ,
ἄλλοι τε κεῖνται πρὸς πύλαις λοχαγέται
πέτροις καταξανθέντες ὀστέων ῥαφάς ;
ἤ νυν φρονεῖν ἄμεινον ἐξαύχει Διός ,
sections.translation: nor should the yawning earth have snatched away the seer , [*] opening wide her mouth to take his chariot and its horses in , nor should the other chieftains be stretched at our gates , their skeletons to atoms crushed ’ neath boulders . Either boast thy wit transcendeth that of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original:
ἢ θεοὺς δικαίως τοὺς κακοὺς ἀπολλύναι .
φιλεῖν μὲν οὖν χρὴ τοὺς σοφοὺς πρῶτον τέκνα ,
ἔπειτα τοκέας πατρίδα θʼ , ἣν αὔξειν χρεὼν
καὶ μὴ κατᾶξαι . σφαλερὸν ἡγεμὼν θρασύς ·
νεώς τε ναύτης ἥσυχος , καιρῷ σοφός .
sections.translation: or else allow that gods are right to slay the ungodly . The wise should love their children first , next their parents and country , whose fortunes it behoves them to increase rather than break down . Rashness in a leader , as in a pilot , causeth shipwreck ; who knoweth when to be quiet is a wise man .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτʼ ἐμοὶ τἀνδρεῖον , ἡ προμηθία .
sections.translation: Yea and this too is bravery , even forethought .

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original:
ἐξαρκέσας ἦν Ζεὺς ὁ τιμωρούμενος ,
ὑμᾶς δʼ ὑβρίζειν οὐκ ἐχρῆν τοιάνδʼ ὕβριν .
sections.translation: The punishment Zeus hath inflicted was surely enough ; there was no need to heap this wanton insult on us .

sections.label: Section 513b
sections.original: ὦ παγκάκιστε —
sections.translation: Abandoned wretch !

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: σῖγʼ , Ἄδραστʼ , ἔχε στόμα ,
sections.translation: Peace , Adrastus ! say no more ; set not thy words before mine ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original:
καὶ μὴ ʼπίπροσθεν τῶν ἐμῶν τοὺς σοὺς λόγους
θῇς · οὐ γὰρ ἥκει πρὸς σὲ κηρύσσων ὅδε ,
ἀλλʼ ὡς ἔμʼ · ἡμᾶς κἀποκρίνασθαι χρεών .
καὶ πρῶτα μέν σε πρὸς τὰ πρῶτʼ ἀμείψομαι .
οὐκ οἶδʼ ἐγὼ Κρέοντα δεσπόζοντʼ ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: for ’ tis not to thee this fellow is come with his message , but to me , and I must answer him . Thy first assertion will I answer first : I am not aware that Creon is my lord and master , or that his power outweigheth mine , that so he should compel

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original:
οὐδὲ σθένοντα μεῖζον , ὥστʼ ἀναγκάσαι
δρᾶν τὰς Ἀθήνας ταῦτʼ · ἄνω γὰρ ἂν ῥέοι
τὰ πράγμαθʼ οὕτως , εἰ ʼπιταξόμεσθα δή .
πόλεμον δὲ τοῦτον οὐκ ἐγὼ καθίσταμαι ,
ὃς οὐδὲ σὺν τοῖσδʼ ἦλθον ἐς Κάδμου χθόνα ·
νεκροὺς δὲ τοὺς θανόντας , οὐ βλάπτων πόλιν
sections.translation: Athens to act on this wise ; nay ! for then would the tide of time have to flow backward , if we are to be ordered about , as he thinks . ’ Tis not I who choose this war , seeing that I did not even join these warriors to go unto the land of Cadmus ; but still I claim to bury the fallen dead , not injuring any state

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original:
οὐδʼ ἀνδροκμῆτας προσφέρων ἀγωνίας ,
θάψαι δικαιῶ , τὸν Πανελλήνων νόμον
σῴζων . τί τούτων ἐστὶν οὐ καλῶς ἔχον ;
εἰ γάρ τι καὶ πεπόνθατʼ Ἀργείων ὕπο ,
sections.translation: nor yet introducing murderous strife , but preserving the law of all Hellas . What is not well in this ? If ye suffered aught from the Argives — lo ! they are dead ; ye took a splendid vengeance on your foes

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original:
τεθνᾶσιν , ἠμύνασθε πολεμίους καλῶς ,
αἰσχρῶς δʼ ἐκείνοις , χἡ δίκη διοίχεται .
ἐάσατʼ ἤδη γῇ καλυφθῆναι νεκρούς ,
ὅθεν δʼ ἕκαστον ἐς τὸ φῶς ἀφίκετο ,
ἐνταῦθʼ ἀπελθεῖν , πνεῦμα μὲν πρὸς αἰθέρα ,
sections.translation: and covered them with shame , and now your right is at an end . Let [*] the dead now be buried in the earth , and each element return [*] to the place from whence it came to the body , the breath to the air , the body to the ground ; for in no wise did we get it

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original:
τὸ σῶμα δʼ ἐς γῆν · οὔτι γὰρ κεκτήμεθα
ἡμέτερον αὐτὸ πλὴν ἐνοικῆσαι βίον ,
κἄπειτα τὴν θρέψασαν αὐτὸ δεῖ λαβεῖν .
δοκεῖς κακουργεῖν Ἄργος οὐ θάπτων νεκρούς ;
ἥκιστα · πάσης Ἑλλάδος κοινὸν τόδε ,
sections.translation: for our own , but to live our life in , and after that its mother earth must take it back again . Dost think ’ tis Argos thou art injuring in refusing burial to the dead ? Nay ! all Hellas shares herein , if a man rob the dead of their due

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original:
εἰ τοὺς θανόντας νοσφίσας ὧν χρῆν λαχεῖν
ἀτάφους τις ἕξει · δειλίαν γὰρ ἐσφέρει
τοῖς ἀλκίμοισιν οὗτος ἢν τεθῇ νόμος .
κἀμοὶ μὲν ἦλθες δείνʼ ἀπειλήσων ἔπη ,
νεκροὺς δὲ ταρβεῖτʼ , εἰ κρυβήσονται χθονί ;
sections.translation: and keep them from the tomb ; for , if this law be enacted , it will strike dismay into the stoutest hearts . And art thou come to cast dire threats at me , while thy own folk are afraid of giving burial to the dead ? What is your fear ? Think you they will undermine your land

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original:
τί μὴ γένηται ; μὴ κατασκάψωσι γῆν
ταφέντες ὑμῶν ; ἢ τέκνʼ ἐν μυχῷ χθονὸς
φύσωσιν , ἐξ ὧν εἶσί τις τιμωρία ;
σκαιόν γε τἀνάλωμα τῆς γλώσσης τόδε ,
sections.translation: in their graves , or that they will beget children in the womb of earth , from whom shall rise an avenger ? A silly waste of words , in truth it was , to show your fear of paltry groundless terrors .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: φόβους πονηροὺς καὶ κενοὺς δεδοικέναι .
sections.translation: Go , triflers , learn the lesson of human misery ;

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original:
ἀλλʼ , ὦ μάταιοι , γνῶτε τἀνθρώπων κακά ·
παλαίσμαθʼ ἡμῶν ὁ βίος · εὐτυχοῦσι δὲ
οἳ μὲν τάχʼ , οἳ δʼ ἐσαῦθις , οἳ δʼ ἤδη βροτῶν ,
τρυφᾷ δʼ ὁ δαίμων · πρός τε γὰρ τοῦ δυστυχοῦς ,
ὡς εὐτυχήσῃ , τίμιος γεραίρεται ,
sections.translation: our life is made up of struggles ; some men there be that find their fortune soon , others have to wait , while some at once are blest . Fortune lives a dainty life ; to her the wretched pays his court and homage to win her smile ; her likewise doth the prosperous man extol , for fear the favouring gale

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original:
ὅ τʼ ὄλβιός νιν πνεῦμα δειμαίνων λιπεῖν
ὑψηλὸν αἴρει . γνόντας οὖν χρεὼν τάδε
ἀδικουμένους τε μέτρια μὴ θυμῷ φέρειν
ἀδικεῖν τε τοιαῦθʼ οἷα μὴ βλάψαι πόλιν .
πῶς οὖν ἂν εἴη ; τοὺς ὀλωλότας νεκροὺς
sections.translation: may leave him . These lessons should we take to heart , to bear with moderation , free from wrath , our wrongs , and do naught to hurt a whole city . What then ? Let us , who will the pious deed perform , bury the corpses of the slain .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original:
θάψαι δὸς ἡμῖν τοῖς θέλουσιν εὐσεβεῖν .
ἢ δῆλα τἀνθένδʼ · εἶμι καὶ θάψω βίᾳ .
οὐ γάρ ποτʼ εἰς Ἕλληνας ἐξοισθήσεται
ὡς εἰς ἔμʼ ἐλθὼν καὶ πόλιν Πανδίονος
sections.translation: Else is the issue clear ; I will go and bury them by force . For never shall it be proclaimed through Hellas that heaven ’ s ancient law was set at naught , when it devolved on me and the city of Pandion .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: νόμος παλαιὸς δαιμόνων διεφθάρη .
sections.translation: Be of good cheer ; for if thou preserve the light of justice ,

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: θάρσει · τὸ γάρ τοι τῆς Δίκης σῴζων φάος
sections.translation: thou shalt escape many a charge that men might urge .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: πολλοὺς ὑπεκφύγοις ἂν ἀνθρώπων ψόγους .
sections.translation: Wilt thou that I sum up in brief all thou wouldst say ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: βούλῃ συνάψω μῦθον ἐν βραχεῖ σέθεν ;
sections.translation: Say what thou wilt ; for thou art not silent as it is .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: λέγʼ , εἴ τι βούλῃ · καὶ γὰρ οὐ σιγηλὸς εἶ .
sections.translation: Thou shalt never take the sons of Argos from our land .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: οὐκ ἄν ποτʼ ἐκ γῆς παῖδας Ἀργείων λάβοις .
sections.translation: Hear , then , my answer too to that , if so thou wilt .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: κἀμοῦ νυν ἀντάκουσον , εἰ βούλῃ , πάλιν .
sections.translation: I will hear thee ; not that I wish it , but I must give thee thy turn .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: κλύοιμʼ ἄν · οὐ γὰρ ἀλλὰ δεῖ δοῦναι μέρος .
sections.translation: I will bury the dead , when from Asopus ’ land I have removed them .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: θάψω νεκροὺς γῆς ἐξελὼν Ἀσωπίας .
sections.translation: First must thou adventure somewhat in the front of war .

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: ἐν ἀσπίσιν σοι πρῶτα κινδυνευτέον .
sections.translation: Many an enterprise and of a different kind have I ere this endured .

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: πολλοὺς ἔτλην δὴ χἁτέρους ἄλλους πόνους .
sections.translation: Wert thou then begotten of thy sire to cope with every foe ?

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ἦ πᾶσιν οὖν σʼ ἔφυσεν ἐξαρκεῖν πατήρ ;
sections.translation: Ay , with all wanton villains ; virtue I punish not .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: ὅσοι γʼ ὑβρισταί · χρηστὰ δʼ οὐ κολάζομεν .
sections.translation: To meddle is aye thy wont and thy city ’ s too .

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: πράσσειν σὺ πόλλʼ εἴωθας ἥ τε σὴ πόλις .
sections.translation: Hence her enterprise on many a held hath won her frequent success .

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original:
τοιγὰρ πονοῦσα πολλὰ πόλλʼ εὐδαιμονεῖ .
ἔλθʼ , ὥς σε λόγχη σπαρτὸς ἐν πόλει λάβῃ .
sections.translation: Come then , that the warriors of the dragon-crop may catch thee in our city .

sections.label: Section 579
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἐκ δράκοντος θοῦρος ἂν γένοιτʼ Ἄρης ;
sections.translation: What furious warrior-host could spring from dragon ’ s seed ?

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: γνώσῃ σὺ πάσχων · νῦν δʼ ἔτʼ εἶ νεανίας .
sections.translation: Thou shalt learn that to thy cost . As yet thou art young and rash .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original:
οὔτοι μʼ ἐπαρεῖς ὥστε θυμῶσαι φρένας
τοῖς σοῖσι κόμποις · ἀλλʼ ἀποστέλλου χθονός ,
λόγους ματαίους οὕσπερ ἠνέγκω λαβών .
περαίνομεν γὰρ οὐδέν .
sections.translation: Thy boastful speech stirs not my heart at all to rage . Yet get thee gone from my land , taking with thee the idle words thou broughtest ; for we are making no advance . [ Exit Herald .]

sections.label: Section 584b
sections.original: ὁρμᾶσθαι χρεὼν
sections.translation: ’ Tis time for all to start ,

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original:
πάντʼ ἄνδρʼ ὁπλίτην ἁρμάτων τʼ ἐπεμβάτην ,
μοναμπύκων τε φάλαρα κινεῖσθαι στόμα
ἀφρῷ καταστάζοντα Καδμείαν χθόνα .
χωρήσομαι γὰρ ἑπτὰ πρὸς Κάδμου πύλας
sections.translation: each stout footman , and whoso mounts the car ; ’ tis time the bit , dripping with foam , should urge the charger on toward the land of Cadmus . For I will march in person to the seven gates thereof

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original:
αὐτὸς σίδηρον ὀξὺν ἐν χεροῖν ἔχων
Ἄδραστε , κἀμοὶ μὴ ἀναμίγνυσθαι τύχας
τὰς σάς . ἐγὼ γὰρ δαίμονος τοὐμοῦ μέτα
στρατηλατήσω καινὸς ἐν καινῷ δορί .
ἓν δεῖ μόνον μοι · τοὺς θεοὺς ἔχειν , ὅσοι
δίκην σέβονται · ταῦτα γὰρ ξυνόνθʼ ὁμοῦ
sections.translation: with the sharp sword in my hand , and be myself my herald . But thee , Adrastus , I bid stay , nor blend with mine thy fortunes , for I will take my own good star to lead my host , a chieftain famed in famous deeds of arms . One thing alone I need , the favour of all gods that reverence right , for the presence of these things

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original:
νίκην δίδωσιν . ἁρετὴ δʼ οὐδὲν λέγει
βροτοῖσιν , ἢ μὴ τὸν θεὸν χρῄζοντʼ ἔχῃ .
sections.translation: insures victory . For their valour availeth men naught , unless they have the god ’ s goodwill . [ Exit Theseus .

sections.label: Section 1.598
sections.original:
— ὦ μέλεαι μελέων ματέρες λοχαγῶν ,
ὥς μοι ὑφʼ ἥπατι χλωρὸν δεῖμα θάσσει —
sections.translation: Unhappy mothers of those hapless chiefs ! How wildly in my heart pale fear stirs up alarm !

sections.label: Section 1.600
sections.original: — τίνʼ αὐδὰν τάνδε προσφέρεις νέαν ;
sections.translation: What is this new cry thou utterest ?

sections.label: Section 1.601
sections.original: — στράτευμα πᾷ Παλλάδος κριθήσεται ;
sections.translation: I fear the issue of the strife , whereto the hosts of Pallas march .

sections.label: Section 1.602
sections.original: — διὰ δορὸς εἶπας , ἢ λόγων ξυναλλαγαῖς ;
sections.translation: Dost speak of issues of the sword , or interchange of words ?

sections.label: Section 1.603
sections.original:
— γένοιτʼ ἂν κέρδος · εἰ δʼ ἀρείφατοι
φόνοι μάχαι στερνοτυπεῖς τʼ ἀνὰ πτόλιν
sections.translation: That last were gain indeed ; but if the carnage of battle , fighting , and

sections.label: Section 1.605
sections.original:
κτύποι φανήσονται , τάλαι-
να , τίνα λόγον , τίνʼ ἂν τῶνδʼ αἰτία λάβοιμι ;
sections.translation: the noise of beaten breasts again be heard in the land , what , alas ! will be said of me , who am the cause thereof ?

sections.label: Section 1.608
sections.original:
— ἀλλὰ τὸν εὐτυχίᾳ λαμπρὸν ἄν τις αἱροῖ
μοῖρα πάλιν · τόδε μοι θράσος ἀμφιβαίνει .
sections.translation: Yet may fate again bring low the brilliant victor ; ’ tis this brave thought that twines about my heart .

sections.label: Section 1.610
sections.original: — δικαίους δαίμονας σύ γʼ ἐννέπεις .
sections.translation: Thou speak ’ st of the gods as if they were just .

sections.label: Section 1.611
sections.original: — τίνες γὰρ ἄλλοι νέμουσι συμφοράς ;
sections.translation: For who but they allot whate ’ er betides ?

sections.label: Section 1.612
sections.original: — διάφορα πολλὰ θεῶν βροτοῖσιν εἰσορῶ .
sections.translation: I see many a contradiction in their dealings with men .

sections.label: Section 1.613
sections.original:
— φόβῳ γὰρ τῷ πάρος διόλλυσαι ·
δίκα δίκαν δʼ ἐκάλεσε καὶ φόνος φόνον ,
sections.translation: The former fear hath warped thy judgment . Vengeance calls vengeance forth ; slaughter calls for slaughter ,

sections.label: Section 1.615
sections.original:
κακῶν δʼ ἀναψυχὰς θεοὶ
βροτοῖς νέμουσι , πάντων τέρμʼ ἔχοντες αὐτοί .
sections.translation: but the gods give respite from affliction , holding in their own hands each thing ’ s allotted end .

sections.label: Section 2.618
sections.original:
— τὰ καλλίπυργα πεδία πῶς ἱκοίμεθʼ ἄν ,
Καλλίχορον θεᾶς ὕδωρ λιποῦσαι —
sections.translation: Would I could reach yon plains with turrets crowned , leaving Callichorus , fountain of the goddess !

sections.label: Section 2.620
sections.original:
— ποτανὰν εἴ σέ τις θεῶν κτίσαι .
— διπόταμον ἵνα πόλιν μόλω .
sections.translation: O that some god would give me wings to fly to the city of rivers twain !

sections.label: Section 2.622
sections.original: — εἰδείης ἂν φίλων εἰδείης ἂν τύχας .
sections.translation: So might ’ st thou see and know the fortunes of thy friends .

sections.label: Section 2.623
sections.original:
— τίς ποτʼ αἶσα , τίς ἄρα πότμος
ἐπιμένει τὸν ἄλκιμον
sections.translation: What fate , what issue there awaits the valiant

sections.label: Section 2.625
sections.original: τᾶσδε γᾶς ἄνακτα ;
sections.translation: monarch of this land ?

sections.label: Section 2.626
sections.original:
— κεκλημένους μὲν ἀνακαλούμεθʼ αὖ θεούς ·
— ἀλλὰ φόβων πίστις ἅδε πρώτα .
sections.translation: Once more do we invoke the gods we called upon before ; yea , in our fear this is our first and chiefest trust .

sections.label: Section 2.628
sections.original:
— ἰὼ Ζεῦ , τᾶς παλαιομάτορος
παιδογόνε πόριος Ἰνάχου .
sections.translation: O Zeus , father to the child the heifer-mother bore in days long past , that daughter of Inachus !

sections.label: Section 2.630
sections.original: — πόλει μοι ξύμμαχος γενοῦ τᾷδʼ εὐμενής .
sections.translation: O be gracious , I pray , and champion this city !

sections.label: Section 2.631
sections.original:
— τὸ σὸν ἄγαλμα , τὸ σὸν ἵδρυμα
πόλεος , ἐκκομίζομαι
πρὸς πυρὰν ὑβρισθέν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis thy own darling , thy own settler in the city of Argos that I [*] am striving to rescue for the funeral pyre from outrageous insult .
